569	    def _execute_gen(self, expr: SglGen):
570	        sampling_params = self._resolve_sampling_params(expr.sampling_params)
571	        name = expr.name
572	        if not self.stream:
573	            if self.num_api_spec_tokens is None:
574	                comp, meta_info = self.backend.generate(
575	                    self,
576	                    sampling_params=sampling_params,
577	                )
578	
579	            else:
580	                if self.backend.is_chat_model:
581	                    # Speculative execution on models with only chat interface.
582	                    # Store the calls into a temporary list.
583	                    # They will be lazily executed later.
584	                    comp, meta_info = self.backend.generate(
585	                        self,
586	                        sampling_params=sampling_params,
587	                        spec_var_name=name,
588	                    )
589	                    return
590	
591	                else:  # Speculative execution on models with completion interface
592	                    comp, meta_info = self._spec_gen(sampling_params)
593	            if isinstance(comp, list):
594	                self.text_ += comp[0]
595	            else:
596	                assert isinstance(comp, str)
597	                self.text_ += comp
598	
599	            self.variables[name] = comp
600	            self.meta_info[name] = meta_info
601	            self.variable_event[name].set()
602	        else:
603	            assert (
604	                self.num_api_spec_tokens is None
605	            ), "stream is not supported with api speculative execution"
606	            generator = self.backend.generate_stream(
607	                self, sampling_params=sampling_params
608	            )
609	
610	            self.variables[name] = ""
611	            self.stream_var_event[name].set()
612	
613	            for comp, meta_info in generator:
614	                self.text_ += comp
615	                self.variables[name] += comp
616	                self.meta_info[name] = meta_info
617	                self.stream_var_event[name].set()
618	                self.stream_text_event.set()
619	
620	            self.variable_event[name].set()
621	            self.stream_var_event[name].set()

624	        choices_decision = self.backend.select(
625	            self, expr.choices, expr.temperature, expr.choices_method
626	        )
627	        if expr.name is not None:
628	            name = expr.name
629	            self.variables[name] = choices_decision.decision

631	            self.variable_event[name].set()

642	        assert self.cur_role is None, "Nested roles are not allowed."
643	
644	        if len(self.messages_) == 0 and expr.role != "system":
645	            # Insert the default system message

647	            if default_system:

652	        self.cur_role = expr.role

654	        prefix, _ = self.chat_template.get_prefix_and_suffix(expr.role, self.messages_)

656	        self._execute_fill(prefix, prefix=True)

20	
21	    def _run_decode_json(
22	        self, json_schema, return_logprob=False, top_logprobs_num=0, n=1
23	    ):
24	        response = requests.post(
25	            self.base_url + "/generate",
26	            json={
27	                "text": (
28	                    "Introduce the capital of France. Return in a JSON format. The JSON Schema is: "

31	                "sampling_params": {
32	                    "temperature": 0 if n == 1 else 0.5,
33	                    "max_new_tokens": 128,
34	                    "n": n,
35	                    "stop_token_ids": [119690],
36	                    "json_schema": json_schema,
37	                },
38	                "stream": False,
39	                "return_logprob": return_logprob,
40	                "top_logprobs_num": top_logprobs_num,
41	                "logprob_start_len": 0,
42	            },
43	        )
44	        ret = response.json()
45	        print(json.dumps(ret))
46	        print("=" * 100)
47	
48	        if not json_schema or json_schema == "INVALID":
49	            return
50	
51	        # Make sure the json output is valid
52	        try:

57	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj["name"], str)

10	from sglang.srt.sampling.custom_logit_processor import CustomLogitProcessor
11	from sglang.srt.sampling.sampling_params import TOP_K_ALL
12	from sglang.srt.server_args import get_global_server_args

21	@dataclasses.dataclass
22	class SamplingBatchInfo:
23	    # Basic batched sampling params
24	    temperatures: torch.Tensor
25	    top_ps: torch.Tensor
26	    top_ks: torch.Tensor
27	    min_ps: torch.Tensor
28	
29	    # Whether all requests use greedy sampling
30	    is_all_greedy: bool
31	
32	    # Whether any requests use top_p sampling
33	    need_top_p_sampling: bool
34	
35	    # Whether any requests use top_k sampling
36	    need_top_k_sampling: bool
37	
38	    # Whether any request needs min_p sampling
39	    need_min_p_sampling: bool
40	
41	    # Masking tensors for grammar-guided structured outputs
42	    vocab_size: int
43	    grammars: Optional[List] = None
44	    vocab_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
45	    apply_mask_func: Optional[Callable[[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor], None]] = None
46	
47	    # Penalizer
48	    penalizer_orchestrator: Optional[penaltylib.BatchedPenalizerOrchestrator] = None
49	    acc_linear_penalties: torch.Tensor = None  # Used in the overlap mode
50	
51	    # Whether any request has custom logit processor
52	    has_custom_logit_processor: bool = False
53	    # Custom parameters
54	    custom_params: Optional[List[Optional[Dict[str, Any]]]] = None
55	    # Custom logit processor
56	    custom_logit_processor: Optional[
57	        Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]
58	    ] = None
59	
60	    # Used for deterministic sampling
61	    sampling_seed: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
62	
63	    # Device
64	    device: str = "cuda"
65	
66	    # Handle logit bias
67	    logit_bias: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
68	
69	    @classmethod
70	    def from_schedule_batch(cls, batch: ScheduleBatch, vocab_size: int):
71	        global_server_args = get_global_server_args()
72	        enable_deterministic = global_server_args.enable_deterministic_inference
73	
74	        reqs = batch.reqs
75	        device = batch.device
76	        temperatures = torch.tensor(
77	            [r.sampling_params.temperature for r in reqs],
78	            dtype=torch.float,
79	            device=device,
80	        ).view(-1, 1)
81	        top_ps = torch.tensor(
82	            [r.sampling_params.top_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
83	        )
84	        top_ks = torch.tensor(
85	            [r.sampling_params.top_k for r in reqs], dtype=torch.int32, device=device
86	        )
87	        min_ps = torch.tensor(
88	            [r.sampling_params.min_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
89	        )
90	        sampling_seed = (
91	            torch.tensor(
92	                [
93	                    (
94	                        r.sampling_params.sampling_seed
95	                        if r.sampling_params.sampling_seed is not None
96	                        else 42
97	                    )
98	                    for r in reqs
99	                ],
100	                dtype=torch.int64,
101	                device=device,
102	            )
103	            if enable_deterministic
104	            else None
105	        )
106	
107	        logit_bias = None
108	        if any(r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None for r in reqs):
109	            logit_bias = torch.zeros(len(reqs), vocab_size, device=device)
110	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
111	                if r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None:
112	                    for key, value in r.sampling_params.logit_bias.items():
113	                        logit_bias[i, int(key)] = value
114	
115	        # Check if any request has custom logit processor
116	        has_custom_logit_processor = (
117	            global_server_args.enable_custom_logit_processor
118	            and any(r.custom_logit_processor for r in reqs)  # check the flag first.
119	        )  # then check the requests.
120	
121	        if has_custom_logit_processor:
122	            # Merge the same type of custom logit processors together
123	            processor_dict = {}
124	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
125	                if r.custom_logit_processor is None:
126	                    continue
127	                processor_str = r.custom_logit_processor
128	                if processor_str not in processor_dict:
129	                    processor_dict[processor_str] = []
130	                processor_dict[processor_str].append(i)
131	
132	            merged_custom_logit_processor = {
133	                hash(processor_str): (
134	                    # The deserialized custom logit processor object
135	                    CustomLogitProcessor.from_str(processor_str),
136	                    # The mask tensor for the requests that use this custom logit processor
137	                    torch.zeros(len(reqs), dtype=torch.bool)
138	                    .scatter_(0, torch.tensor(true_indices), True)
139	                    .to(device, non_blocking=True),
140	                )
141	                for processor_str, true_indices in processor_dict.items()
142	            }
143	            custom_params = [r.sampling_params.custom_params for r in reqs]
144	        else:
145	            merged_custom_logit_processor = None
146	            custom_params = None
147	
148	        # Each penalizers will do nothing if they evaluate themselves as not required by looking at
149	        # the sampling_params of the requests (See {_is_required()} of each penalizers). So this
150	        # should not add hefty computation overhead other than simple checks.

24	class GrammarManager:
25	    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
26	        self.scheduler = scheduler
27	        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
28	        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
29	        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
30	            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
31	                self.server_args,
32	                scheduler.tokenizer,
33	                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
34	                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
35	            )
36	        else:
37	            self.grammar_backend = None
38	
39	        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
40	        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
41	        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
42	        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank
43	
44	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
45	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
46	            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
47	        )
48	
49	    def __len__(self):
50	        return len(self.grammar_queue)
51	
52	    def clear(self):
53	        if self.grammar_backend:
54	            self.grammar_backend.reset()
55	
56	    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
57	        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0
58	
59	    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
60	        for req in self.grammar_queue:
61	            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
62	                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
63	                if req.grammar:
64	                    req.grammar.cancel()
65	                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")
66	
67	    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
68	        # Init grammar cache for this request
69	        add_to_grammar_queue = False
70	        if (
71	            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
72	            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
73	            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
74	            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
75	        ):
76	            if self.grammar_backend is None:
77	                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
78	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
79	            else:
80	                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
81	                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
82	                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
83	                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
84	                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
85	                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
86	                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
87	                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
88	
89	                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
90	                    key, req.require_reasoning
91	                )
92	                req.grammar = value
93	
94	                if not cache_hit:
95	                    req.grammar_key = key
96	                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
97	                else:
98	                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
99	                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
100	                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
101	
102	        if add_to_grammar_queue:
103	            self.grammar_queue.append(req)
104	
105	        return add_to_grammar_queue
106	
107	    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
108	        """
109	        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.
110	
111	        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
112	        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
113	        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)
114	
115	        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
116	        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
117	        """
118	        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
119	        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
120	
121	        # Poll for ready requests
122	        start_time = time.perf_counter()
123	        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
124	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
125	                if i in ready_req_idxs:
126	                    continue
127	
128	                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
129	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
130	                    continue
131	
132	                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
133	                if req.grammar.done():
134	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
135	
136	            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
137	            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)
138	
139	        # Check failed requests
140	        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
141	            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
142	                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
143	                if (
144	                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
145	                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
146	                ):
147	                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
148	                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
149	                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)

203	        # Find a grammar from the list
204	        first_grammar = next(grammar for grammar in self.grammars if grammar)
205	
206	        # TODO(lianmin): Maybe we can reuse the existing mask?
207	        self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(
208	            vocab_size=self.vocab_size,
209	            batch_size=len(self.temperatures),
210	            device=self.device,
211	        )
212	        self.apply_mask_func = (
213	            first_grammar.apply_vocab_mask
214	        )  # force to use static method
215	
216	        # Apply the mask
217	        for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
218	            if grammar and not grammar.finished and not grammar.is_terminated():
219	                grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)
220	
221	        # Move the mask to the device if needed
222	        self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.move_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, self.device)
223	
224	    def update_penalties(self):
225	        if self.penalizer_orchestrator.is_required:
226	            self.acc_linear_penalties = torch.zeros(
227	                (len(self.temperatures), self.vocab_size),
228	                dtype=torch.float32,
229	                device=self.temperatures.device,
230	            )
231	            self.penalizer_orchestrator.apply(self.acc_linear_penalties)
232	        else:
233	            self.acc_linear_penalties = None
234	
235	    def apply_logits_bias(self, logits: torch.Tensor):
236	        if self.acc_linear_penalties is not None:
237	            # Used in the overlap mode
238	            logits.add_(self.acc_linear_penalties)
239	
240	        if self.penalizer_orchestrator and self.penalizer_orchestrator.is_required:
241	            # Used in the non-overlap mode
242	            self.penalizer_orchestrator.apply(logits)
243	
244	        if self.vocab_mask is not None:
245	            self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
246	
247	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
248	            logits.add_(self.logit_bias)
249	
250	    def filter_batch(self, keep_indices: List[int], keep_indices_device: torch.Tensor):
251	        self.penalizer_orchestrator.filter(keep_indices_device)
252	
253	        if self.has_custom_logit_processor:
254	            self._filter_batch_custom_logit_processor(keep_indices, keep_indices_device)
255	
256	        for item in [
257	            "temperatures",
258	            "top_ps",
259	            "top_ks",
260	            "min_ps",
261	            "sampling_seed",
262	        ]:
263	            value = getattr(self, item, None)
264	            if value is not None:
265	                setattr(self, item, value[keep_indices_device])
266	
267	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
268	            self.logit_bias = self.logit_bias[keep_indices_device]
269	
270	    def _filter_batch_custom_logit_processor(
271	        self, keep_indices: List[int], keep_indices_device: torch.Tensor
272	    ):
273	        """Filter the custom logit processor and custom params"""
274	        self.custom_logit_processor = {
275	            k: (p, mask[keep_indices_device])
276	            for k, (p, mask) in self.custom_logit_processor.items()
277	            if torch.any(
278	                mask[keep_indices_device]
279	            )  # ignore the custom logit processor whose mask is all False
280	        }
281	        self.custom_params = [self.custom_params[i] for i in keep_indices]
282	
283	        # If the custom logit processor is an empty dict, set the flag to False,
284	        # and set the custom logit processor and custom params to None.
285	        if len(self.custom_logit_processor) == 0:
286	            self.custom_logit_processor = None
287	            self.custom_params = None
288	            self.has_custom_logit_processor = False
289	
290	    @staticmethod
291	    def merge_custom_logit_processor(
292	        lhs: Optional[Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]],
293	        rhs: Optional[Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]],
294	        bs1: int,
295	        bs2: int,
296	        device: str,
297	    ):
298	        if lhs is None and rhs is None:
299	            return None
300	        lhs, rhs = lhs or {}, rhs or {}
301	
302	        keys = set(lhs.keys()).union(set(rhs.keys()))
303	        merged_dict = {}
304	
305	        for k in keys:
306	            # Get the logit processor object
307	            processor = lhs[k][0] if k in lhs else rhs[k][0]
308	            # Get and merge the mask tensors from the two dicts
309	            left_mask = (
310	                lhs[k][1]
311	                if k in lhs
312	                else torch.zeros(bs1, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
313	            )
314	            right_mask = (
315	                rhs[k][1]
316	                if k in rhs
317	                else torch.zeros(bs2, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
318	            )
319	            merged_dict[k] = (processor, torch.cat([left_mask, right_mask]))
320	
321	            assert merged_dict[k][1].shape[0] == bs1 + bs2, (
322	                f"The batch size of merged mask ({merged_dict[k][1].shape[0]}) does not match "
323	                f"the sum of the batch sizes of the two masks ({bs1 + bs2})"
324	                f"\n{left_mask=}\n{right_mask=}\n{bs1=}\n{bs2=}"
325	                f"\n{lhs=}\n{rhs=}"
326	            )
327	
328	        return merged_dict
329	
330	    def merge_batch(self, other: "SamplingBatchInfo"):
331	        self.penalizer_orchestrator.merge(other.penalizer_orchestrator)
332	
333	        # Merge the custom logit processors and custom params lists
334	        if self.has_custom_logit_processor or other.has_custom_logit_processor:
335	            # Merge the custom logit processors
336	            self.custom_logit_processor = (
337	                SamplingBatchInfo.merge_custom_logit_processor(
338	                    self.custom_logit_processor,
339	                    other.custom_logit_processor,
340	                    len(self),
341	                    len(other),
342	                    self.device,
343	                )
344	            )
345	            # Merge the custom params lists
346	            self.custom_params = self.custom_params or [None] * len(self)
347	            other.custom_params = other.custom_params or [None] * len(other)
348	            self.custom_params.extend(other.custom_params)
349	
350	            # Set the flag to True if any of the two has custom logit processor
351	            self.has_custom_logit_processor = True
352	
353	        # Merge logit bias - note this has to come before the temperatures tensor update! Otherwise will cause crashes.
354	        # See note below on len(self) and len(other).
355	        self.logit_bias = merge_bias_tensor(
356	            self.logit_bias, other.logit_bias, len(self), len(other), self.device, 0.0
357	        )
358	
359	        # Note: because the __len()__ operator is defined on the temperatures tensor,
360	        # please make sure any merge operation with len(self) or len(other) is done before
361	        # the merge operation of the temperatures tensor below.
362	        for item in [
363	            "temperatures",
364	            "top_ps",
365	            "top_ks",
366	            "min_ps",
367	            "sampling_seed",
368	        ]:
369	            self_val = getattr(self, item, None)
370	            other_val = getattr(other, item, None)
371	            if self_val is not None and other_val is not None:
372	                setattr(self, item, torch.cat([self_val, other_val]))
373	
374	        self.is_all_greedy &= other.is_all_greedy
375	        self.need_top_p_sampling |= other.need_top_p_sampling
376	        self.need_top_k_sampling |= other.need_top_k_sampling
377	        self.need_min_p_sampling |= other.need_min_p_sampling
378	
379	        self.adjusted_merge_batch(other)
380	
381	    def copy_for_forward(self):
382	        # Accumulate the penalty into a pre-allocated buffer to get rid of the dependency of `penalizer_orchestrator` later
383	        self.update_penalties()
384	        return dataclasses.replace(self, penalizer_orchestrator=None)
385

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive report.

## Comprehensive Report: SGLang Output Constraints Implementation

Based on my thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase, here's a complete analysis of how output constraints are implemented:

---

### 1. USER-FACING API - How Users Call `gen()` with Constraints

**File:** `/python/sglang/lang/api.py` (lines 75-139)

The primary user interface is the `gen()` function which accepts three constraint parameters:

```python
def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    ...
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
    ...
) -> SglExpr:
```

**Key flow in `gen()`:**
- If `choices` is provided, returns `SglSelect` object
- If `regex` is provided, validates it with `re.compile(regex)`
- Returns `SglGen` object containing `SglSamplingParams` with the constraint parameters

---

### 2. CONSTRAINT DATA REPRESENTATION - SglSamplingParams Class

**File:** `/python/sglang/lang/ir.py` (lines 18-139)

The `SglSamplingParams` dataclass stores all sampling parameters including constraints:

```python
@dataclasses.dataclass
class SglSamplingParams:
    max_new_tokens: int = 128
    ...
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None
    dtype: Optional[str] = None
    regex: Optional[str] = None
    # Also has methods to_openai_kwargs(), to_srt_kwargs(), etc.
```

---

### 3. SAMPLING PARAMS AT SERVER LEVEL

**File:** `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` (lines 31-96)

Server-side `SamplingParams` class mirrors the constraint parameters:

```python
class SamplingParams:
    def __init__(
        self,
        ...
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
        ...
    ):
        self.json_schema = json_schema
        self.regex = regex
        self.ebnf = ebnf
        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
```

**Supported constraint types:**
- `json_schema`: JSON Schema string
- `regex`: Regular expression pattern
- `ebnf`: Extended Backus-Naur Form grammar
- `structural_tag`: Structural tag format

---

### 4. CONSTRAINT BASE CLASS & INTERFACE

**File:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` (lines 42-118)

`BaseGrammarObject` is the abstract base class for all constraint implementations:

```python
class BaseGrammarObject:
    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None:
        """Accept a token in the grammar."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    def allocate_vocab_mask(self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device) -> torch.Tensor:
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    @staticmethod
    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
        """Try to jump forward in the grammar."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    @property
    def finished(self):
        return self._finished
```

---

### 5. GRAMMAR BACKEND REGISTRATION & CREATION

**File:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` (lines 199-275)

Grammar backends are registered and created through a factory pattern:

```python
GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}

def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func

def create_grammar_backend(
    server_args: ServerArgs,
    tokenizer,
    vocab_size: int,
    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
    name = server_args.grammar_backend
    
    if name == "outlines":
        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(...)
    elif name == "xgrammar":
        grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(...)
    elif name == "llguidance":
        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(...)
    elif name == "none":
        return None
```

**Supported backends:**
1. **xgrammar** - High-performance grammar-based constrained decoding
2. **outlines** - Regex/JSON schema support via Outlines library
3. **llguidance** - LLGuidance-based constrained decoding
4. **none** - No constraint support

---

### 6. CONSTRAINT BACKEND IMPLEMENTATIONS

#### 6.1 XGrammar Backend
**File:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py` (lines 175-321)

```python
class XGrammarGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
        # Compiles JSON schema to XGrammar compiled grammar
        ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(
            schema=key_string, any_whitespace=self.any_whitespace
        )
    
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
        # Compiles regex pattern to XGrammar compiled grammar
        ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_regex(key_string)
    
    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
        # Compiles EBNF grammar to XGrammar compiled grammar
        ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_grammar(key_string)
    
    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
        # Compiles structural tags (XML/JSON-like formats)
        ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_structural_tag(key_string)
```

**XGrammarGrammar class** (lines 53-167):
- Wraps XGrammar's `GrammarMatcher`
- Manages token acceptance and rollback
- Generates vocabulary masks for constrained sampling

#### 6.2 Outlines Backend
**File:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py` (lines 114-179)

```python
class OutlinesGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
        # Converts JSON schema to regex via build_regex_from_schema
        regex = build_regex_from_object(key_string, whitespace_pattern=...)
        return self._compile_regex(regex)
    
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str):
        # Compiles regex using Outlines RegexGuide
        guide = RegexGuide.from_regex(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
```

---

### 7. CONSTRAINT FLOW: Grammar Manager

**File:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` (lines 24-196)

The `GrammarManager` orchestrates constraint processing:

```python
def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
    # Detects constraint type from sampling_params
    if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
        key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
    elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
        key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
    elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
        key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
    elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
        key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
    
    # Gets or creates grammar object via backend
    value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
        key, req.require_reasoning
    )
    req.grammar = value
```

**Key features:**
- Caching of compiled grammars (same constraint reused)
- Asynchronous grammar compilation via ThreadPoolExecutor
- Distributed synchronization across ranks (for multi-GPU)

---

### 8. CONSTRAINT ENFORCEMENT DURING DECODING

**File:** `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_batch_info.py` (lines 197-245)

Constraints are applied to logits during token sampling:

```python
def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):
    # Allocate vocabulary mask tensor
    self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(
        vocab_size=self.vocab_size,
        batch_size=len(self.temperatures),
        device=self.device,
    )
    
    # Fill mask for each request in batch
    for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
        if grammar and not grammar.finished:
            grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)

def apply_logits_bias(self, logits: torch.Tensor):
    # Apply vocabulary mask to logits
    if self.vocab_mask is not None:
        self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
```

**Constraint enforcement flow:**
1. **Allocate**: Create bitmask tensor (batch_size × vocab_size)
2. **Fill**: For each request, set valid tokens to True
3. **Apply**: Set invalid tokens' logits to -∞ (via masking or bitmask)
4. **Accept**: After sampling, call `grammar.accept_token(token_id)`

---

**EBNF Test:** `/python/sglang/test/kits/ebnf_constrained_kit.py`

---

### 11. CONSTRAINT EXPORTS

**File:** `/python/sglang/__init__.py`

Public API exports:
- `gen` - Main function for generation with constraints
- `select` - For choice constraints
- Three choice sampling methods:
  - `token_length_normalized`
  - `greedy_token_selection`
  - `unconditional_likelihood_normalized`

---

### Summary Flow Diagram

```
User Code
  ↓
sglang.gen(regex=..., json_schema=..., ebnf=...)
  ↓
SglGen(SglSamplingParams) → SglExpr
  ↓
Interpreter → Backend (Runtime/Engine)
  ↓
SamplingParams (server-side) → GrammarManager
  ↓
Grammar Backend (xgrammar/outlines/llguidance)
  ↓
Grammar Compilation & Caching
  ↓
Vocab Mask Generation (per batch)
  ↓
SamplingBatchInfo.fill_vocab_mask() → sets valid tokens
  ↓
Logit Masking: apply_vocab_mask(logits, vocab_mask)
  ↓
Token Sampling (only valid tokens can be selected)
  ↓
Grammar.accept_token() → update grammar state
  ↓
Generate next token until grammar terminates or max_tokens reached
```

---

### Key Architecture Notes

1. **Multi-backend support**: Pluggable architecture allows different constraint engines
2. **Lazy compilation**: Grammars compiled asynchronously and cached
3. **Batch efficiency**: Vocabulary masks computed once per batch, applied to all requests
4. **Device agnostic**: Masks can be on CPU/CUDA with appropriate ops (Triton or HIP kernels)
5. **Distributed ready**: Grammar sync across distributed ranks
6. **Rollback support**: Grammars can rollback tokens (for re-tokenization after jumps)

## Comprehensive Report: SGLang Output Constraints Implementation

Based on my thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase, here's a complete analysis of how output constraints are implemented:

---

### 1. USER-FACING API - How Users Call `gen()` with Constraints

**File:** `/python/sglang/lang/api.py` (lines 75-139)

The primary user interface is the `gen()` function which accepts three constraint parameters:

```python
def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    ...
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
    ...
) -> SglExpr:
```

**Key flow in `gen()`:**
- If `choices` is provided, returns `SglSelect` object
- If `regex` is provided, validates it with `re.compile(regex)`
- Returns `SglGen` object containing `SglSamplingParams` with the constraint parameters

---

### 2. CONSTRAINT DATA REPRESENTATION - SglSamplingParams Class

**File:** `/python/sglang/lang/ir.py` (lines 18-139)

The `SglSamplingParams` dataclass stores all sampling parameters including constraints:

```python
@dataclasses.dataclass
class SglSamplingParams:
    max_new_tokens: int = 128
    ...
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None
    dtype: Optional[str] = None
    regex: Optional[str] = None
    # Also has methods to_openai_kwargs(), to_srt_kwargs(), etc.
```

---

### 3. SAMPLING PARAMS AT SERVER LEVEL

**File:** `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` (lines 31-96)

```python

    def __init__(
        self,
        ...
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
        ...
    ):
        self.json_schema = json_schema
        self.regex = regex
        self.ebnf = ebnf
        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
```

**Supported constraint types:**
- `json_schema`: JSON Schema string
- `regex`: Regular expression pattern
- `ebnf`: Extended Backus-Naur Form grammar
- `structural_tag`: Structural tag format

---

### 4. CONSTRAINT BASE CLASS & INTERFACE

**File:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` (lines 42-118)

`BaseGrammarObject` is the abstract base class for all constraint implementations:

```python
class BaseGrammarObject:
    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None:
        """Accept a token in the grammar."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    def allocate_vocab_mask(self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device) -> torch.Tensor:
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    @staticmethod
    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
        """Try to jump forward in the grammar."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    @property
    def finished(self):
        return self._finished
```

---

### 5. GRAMMAR BACKEND REGISTRATION & CREATION

**File:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` (lines 199-275)

Grammar backends are registered and created through a factory pattern:

```python
GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}

def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func

def create_grammar_backend(
    server_args: ServerArgs,
    tokenizer,
    vocab_size: int,
    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
    name = server_args.grammar_backend
    
    if name == "outlines":
        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(...)
    elif name == "xgrammar":
        grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(...)
    elif name == "llguidance":
        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(...)
    elif name == "none":
        return None
```

**Supported backends:**
1. **xgrammar** - High-performance grammar-based constrained decoding
2. **outlines** - Regex/JSON schema support via Outlines library
3. **llguidance** - LLGuidance-based constrained decoding
4. **none** - No constraint support

## SGLang Codebase Structure Analysis

Based on my exploration of the SGLang codebase, here's a comprehensive overview of the structure and constraint handling flow:

### 1. TOP-LEVEL DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

```
/python
├── sglang/                          # Main package
│   ├── __init__.py                 # Public API exports
│   ├── lang/                       # Frontend language DSL
│   │   ├── api.py                  # gen(), gen_int(), gen_string() functions
│   │   ├── ir.py                   # Intermediate representation (SglGen, SglSamplingParams)
│   │   ├── interpreter.py          # StreamExecutor - executes SGL programs
│   │   ├── backend/                # Multiple backend implementations
│   │   └── choices.py              # Choice selection methods
│   ├── srt/                        # Serving Runtime (backend execution)
│   │   ├── sampling/
│   │   │   └── sampling_params.py  # SamplingParams class
│   │   ├── constrained/            # Constraint decoding backends
│   │   │   ├── grammar_manager.py  # GrammarManager - coordinates constraint processing
│   │   │   ├── base_grammar_backend.py
│   │   │   ├── xgrammar_backend.py
│   │   │   ├── outlines_backend.py
│   │   │   └── llguidance_backend.py
│   │   ├── function_call/          # JSON schema handling for tool use
│   │   │   └── function_call_parser.py
│   │   ├── managers/               # Request/scheduler management
│   │   │   ├── scheduler.py        # Main scheduler
│   │   │   ├── schedule_batch.py   # Req class definition
│   │   │   └── io_struct.py        # I/O structures
│   │   ├── server_args.py          # ServerArgs configuration
│   │   └── entrypoints/
│   │       ├── engine.py           # Engine API
│   │       └── openai/             # OpenAI compatible API
│   ├── test/                       # Tests
│   │   ├── kits/
│   │   │   ├── json_constrained_kit.py
│   │   │   ├── regex_constrained_kit.py
│   │   │   └── ebnf_constrained_kit.py
│   │   └── registered/
│   │       ├── function_call/test_json_schema_constraint.py
│   │       └── constrained_decoding/test_constrained_decoding.py
│   └── utils.py

/benchmark
├── json_schema/
└── [other benchmarks]

/test
├── registered/
│   ├── constrained_decoding/
│   ├── function_call/
│   └── spec/
└── [other tests]
```

---

### 2. MAIN PACKAGE STRUCTURE & EXPORTS (`sglang/__init__.py`)

The main SGLang package exports these key APIs:

```python
# Frontend Language APIs
from sglang.lang.api import (
    gen,                    # Main generation function
    gen_int,               # Integer generation
    gen_string,            # String generation
    function,              # Function decorator
    Engine, Runtime,       # Backend runtimes
    select,                # Choice selection
)

# Core concepts exported:
- Engine / Runtime / RuntimeEndpoint  # Backend execution engines
- global_config                       # Global configuration
- ServerArgs                          # Server configuration (lazy import)
```

---

### 3. GEN() FUNCTION SIGNATURE & IMPLEMENTATION

**Location:** `/python/sglang/lang/api.py` (lines 75-139)

```python
def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    min_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    n: Optional[int] = None,
    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,  # CONSTRAINT
):
```

**Key Constraint Parameters:**
- `regex` - Regular expression constraint for output
- `json_schema` - JSON schema constraint for structured output
- `choices` - Limited choice selection
- `dtype` - Output data type (int, str)

**How it works:**
1. If `choices` provided → returns `SglSelect` (special case)
2. Otherwise → creates `SglGen` object with `SglSamplingParams`

```python
def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
    # Check if request has constraints
    if (
        req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
        or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
        or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
        or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
    ):
        # Determine constraint type
        if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
            key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
        elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
            key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
        # ... etc ...
        
        # Get or compile grammar (with caching)
        value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
            key, req.require_reasoning
        )
        req.grammar = value
        
        if not cache_hit:
            # Add to queue for asynchronous grammar compilation
            self.grammar_queue.append(req)
```

- `outlines_backend.py` - Outlines library
- `llguidance_backend.py` - llguidance library
- `base_grammar_backend.py` - Base interface

```
User Code (gen() call)
    ↓
sglang.lang.api.gen()  [Creates SglGen IR with constraints]
    ↓
SglGen.sampling_params  [Contains json_schema/regex/ebnf]
    ↓
StreamExecutor._execute_gen()  [Frontend interpreter]
    ↓

    ↓

    ↓

    ↓
grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value()  [Compiles grammar]
    ↓
req.grammar = (compiled FSM/matcher)  [Attached to request]
    ↓
Sampling Loop (nucleus/topk sampling with grammar filtering)
    ↓
Only tokens matching constraint FSM are sampled
    ↓
Constrained output returned to user
```

### 8. SERVER CONFIGURATION FOR CONSTRAINTS

**File:** `/python/sglang/srt/server_args.py` (lines 275+)

```python
    grammar_backend: str = "xgrammar"  # Options: xgrammar, outlines, llguidance, none
    
    # JSON constraint options

    constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace: bool = False
    
    # Memory/scheduling for constraint processing
    max_running_requests: Optional[int] = None
    schedule_policy: str = "fcfs"

```

---

### 9. KEY ARCHITECTURAL INSIGHTS

1. **Two-tier constraint handling:**
   - **Frontend:** Constraints stored in `SglSamplingParams` as strings (json_schema, regex, ebnf)
   - **Backend:** Grammar objects compiled asynchronously and cached

2. **Asynchronous grammar compilation:**
   - Grammar queue in `GrammarManager` processes constraints off the critical path
   - Requests with uncompiled grammars wait in a separate queue
   - Caching prevents recompilation of identical constraints

3. **Mutually exclusive constraints:**
   - Only ONE of: `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`, `structural_tag` per generation
   - Validated in `SamplingParams.verify()`

4. **Multiple backend support:**
   - Pluggable grammar backends (xgrammar, outlines, llguidance)
   - Each implements same interface for grammar compilation and filtering

5. **Function call tool integration:**
   - Tool schemas converted to JSON schema constraints
   - Supports `tool_choice` semantics (required, auto, specific)
   - Handles complex schemas with `$defs` and `$ref`

---

### 10. EXAMPLE CODE FLOW: JSON SCHEMA CONSTRAINT

```python
# User code
@sgl.function
def my_function(s):
    s += "Generate a city in JSON format"
    s += sgl.gen(
        "result",
        json_schema=json.dumps({
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                "name": {"type": "string"},
                "population": {"type": "integer"}
            },
            "required": ["name", "population"]
        })
    )

# Execution flow:
1. gen() → SglGen(json_schema="...") [Frontend IR]
2. StreamExecutor._execute_gen() → backend.generate(sampling_params)
3. SamplingParams(json_schema="...") [SRT layer]
4. GrammarManager.process_req_with_grammar() [Identify JSON constraint]
5. grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(("json", "..."))
6. XGrammarBackend compiles JSON schema → FSM
7. Sampling loop queries FSM for allowed tokens at each step
8. Output guaranteed to match JSON schema
```

---

This structure demonstrates SGLang's modular design with clear separation between the user-facing API, interpretation, and backend execution, with sophisticated constraint handling infrastructure integrated throughout.

23	def function(

25	):

53	def flush_cache(backend: Optional[BaseBackend] = None):
54	    backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
55	    if backend is None:
56	        return False
57	
58	    # If backend is Runtime
59	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
60	        backend = backend.endpoint
61	    return backend.flush_cache()

65	    backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
66	    if backend is None:
67	        return None
68	
69	    # If backend is Runtime
70	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
71	        backend = backend.endpoint
72	    return backend.get_server_info()

75	def gen(
76	    name: Optional[str] = None,
77	    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
78	    min_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
79	    n: Optional[int] = None,
80	    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
81	    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
82	    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
83	    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
84	    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
85	    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
86	    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
87	    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
88	    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
89	    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
90	    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
91	    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
92	    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
93	    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
94	    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,
95	    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
96	    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
97	    regex: Optional[str] = None,
98	    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
99	):
100	    """Call the model to generate. See the meaning of the arguments in docs/backend/sampling_params.md"""
101	
102	    if choices:
103	        return SglSelect(
104	            name,
105	            choices,
106	            0.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
107	            token_length_normalized if choices_method is None else choices_method,
108	        )
109	
110	    # check regex is valid
111	    if regex is not None:
112	        try:
113	            re.compile(regex)
114	        except re.error as e:
115	            raise e
116	
117	    return SglGen(
118	        name,
119	        max_tokens,
120	        min_tokens,
121	        n,
122	        stop,
123	        stop_token_ids,
124	        stop_regex,
125	        temperature,
126	        top_p,

128	        min_p,
129	        frequency_penalty,

132	        return_logprob,

134	        top_logprobs_num,
135	        return_text_in_logprobs,
136	        dtype,

139	    )

3	import dataclasses
4	import inspect
5	import warnings
6	from typing import List, Optional, Union
7	
8	from sglang.global_config import global_config
9	from sglang.lang.choices import ChoicesSamplingMethod

11	REGEX_INT = r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
12	REGEX_FLOAT = r"[-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
13	REGEX_BOOL = r"(True|False)"
14	REGEX_STR = r"\"[\w\d\s]*\""  # bugs with regex r"\".*\"" in interegular pkg

17	@dataclasses.dataclass
18	class SglSamplingParams:
19	    max_new_tokens: int = 128
20	    min_new_tokens: int = 0
21	    n: int = 1
22	    stop: Union[str, List[str]] = ()
23	    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = ()
24	    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = ()
25	    temperature: float = 1.0
26	    top_p: float = 1.0
27	    top_k: int = -1  # -1 means disable
28	    min_p: float = 0.0
29	    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
30	    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
31	    ignore_eos: bool = False
32	    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None
33	    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = (None,)
34	    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = (None,)
35	    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = (None,)
36	    json_schema: Optional[str] = None
37	
38	    # for constrained generation, not included in to_xxx_kwargs
39	    dtype: Optional[str] = None
40	    regex: Optional[str] = None
41	
42	    def clone(self):
43	        return SglSamplingParams(
44	            self.max_new_tokens,
45	            self.min_new_tokens,
46	            self.n,
47	            self.stop,
48	            self.stop_token_ids,
49	            self.stop_regex,
50	            self.temperature,
51	            self.top_p,
52	            self.top_k,
53	            self.min_p,
54	            self.frequency_penalty,
55	            self.presence_penalty,
56	            self.ignore_eos,
57	            self.return_logprob,
58	            self.logprob_start_len,
59	            self.top_logprobs_num,
60	            self.return_text_in_logprobs,
61	            self.json_schema,
62	        )
63	
64	    def to_openai_kwargs(self):
65	        # OpenAI does not support top_k, so we drop it here
66	        if self.regex is not None:
67	            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the OpenAI backend.")
68	        return {
69	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
70	            "max_completion_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
71	            "n": self.n,
72	            "stop": self.stop or None,
73	            "temperature": self.temperature,
74	            "top_p": self.top_p,
75	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
76	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
77	        }
78	
79	    def to_vertexai_kwargs(self):
80	        if self.regex is not None:
81	            warnings.warn(
82	                "Regular expression is not supported in the VertexAI backend."
83	            )
84	        return {
85	            "candidate_count": 1,
86	            "max_output_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
87	            "stop_sequences": self.stop,
88	            "temperature": self.temperature,
89	            "top_p": self.top_p,
90	            "top_k": self.top_k if self.top_k > 0 else None,
91	        }
92	
93	    def to_anthropic_kwargs(self):
94	        # Anthropic does not support frequency_penalty or presence_penalty, so we drop it here
95	        if self.regex is not None:
96	            warnings.warn(
97	                "Regular expression is not supported in the Anthropic backend."
98	            )
99	        return {
100	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
101	            "stop_sequences": (
102	                self.stop if isinstance(self.stop, (list, tuple)) else [self.stop]
103	            ),
104	            "temperature": self.temperature,
105	            "top_p": self.top_p,
106	            "top_k": self.top_k,
107	        }
108	
109	    def to_litellm_kwargs(self):
110	        if self.regex is not None:
111	            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the LiteLLM backend.")
112	        return {
113	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
114	            "stop": self.stop or None,
115	            "temperature": self.temperature,
116	            "top_p": self.top_p,
117	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
118	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
119	        }
120	
121	    def to_srt_kwargs(self):
122	        return {
123	            "max_new_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
124	            "min_new_tokens": self.min_new_tokens,
125	            "n": self.n,
126	            "stop": self.stop,
127	            "stop_token_ids": self.stop_token_ids,
128	            "stop_regex": self.stop_regex,
129	            "temperature": self.temperature,
130	            "top_p": self.top_p,
131	            "top_k": self.top_k,
132	            "min_p": self.min_p,
133	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
134	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
135	            "ignore_eos": self.ignore_eos,
136	            "regex": self.regex,

141	class SglFunction:
142	    def __init__(self, func, num_api_spec_tokens=None, bind_arguments=None):
143	        self.func = func
144	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
145	        self.bind_arguments = bind_arguments or {}
146	        self.pin_prefix_rid = None
147	
148	        # Parse arguments
149	        argspec = inspect.getfullargspec(func)
150	        assert argspec.args[0] == "s", 'The first argument must be "s"'
151	        self.arg_names = argspec.args[1:]
152	        self.arg_defaults = argspec.defaults if argspec.defaults is not None else []
153	
154	    def bind(self, **kwargs):
155	        assert all(key in self.arg_names for key in kwargs)
156	
157	        new_bind_dict = {**self.bind_arguments, **kwargs}
158	        return SglFunction(self.func, bind_arguments=new_bind_dict)
159	
160	    def run(
161	        self,
162	        *args,
163	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
164	        n: int = 1,
165	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
166	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
167	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
168	        temperature: float = 1.0,
169	        top_p: float = 1.0,
170	        top_k: int = -1,
171	        min_p: float = 0.0,
172	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
173	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
174	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
175	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
176	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
177	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
178	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
179	        stream: bool = False,
180	        backend=None,
181	        use_thread: bool = True,
182	        **kwargs,
183	    ):
184	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program
185	
186	        # avoid using [] as the default arg: https://nikos7am.com/posts/mutable-default-arguments/
187	        if stop is None:
188	            stop = []
189	        if stop_token_ids is None:
190	            stop_token_ids = []
191	        if stop_regex is None:
192	            stop_regex = []
193	
194	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
195	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
196	            n=n,
197	            stop=stop,
198	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
199	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
200	            temperature=temperature,
201	            top_p=top_p,
202	            top_k=top_k,
203	            min_p=min_p,
204	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
205	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
206	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
207	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
208	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
209	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
210	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
211	        )
212	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
213	        return run_program(
214	            self,
215	            backend,
216	            args,
217	            kwargs,
218	            default_sampling_para,
219	            stream,
220	            use_thread=use_thread,
221	        )
222	
223	    def run_batch(
224	        self,
225	        batch_kwargs,
226	        *,
227	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
228	        n: int = 1,
229	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
230	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
231	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
232	        temperature: float = 1.0,
233	        top_p: float = 1.0,
234	        top_k: int = -1,
235	        min_p: float = 0.0,
236	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
237	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
238	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
239	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
240	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
241	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
242	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
243	        backend=None,
244	        num_threads: Union[str, int] = "auto",
245	        progress_bar: bool = False,
246	        generator_style: bool = False,
247	    ):
248	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program_batch
249	
250	        if stop is None:
251	            stop = []
252	        if stop_token_ids is None:
253	            stop_token_ids = []
254	        if stop_regex is None:
255	            stop_regex = []
256	
257	        assert isinstance(batch_kwargs, (list, tuple))
258	        if len(batch_kwargs) == 0:
259	            return []
260	        if not isinstance(batch_kwargs[0], dict):
261	            num_programs = len(batch_kwargs)
262	            # change the list of argument values to dict of arg_name -> arg_value
263	            batch_kwargs = [
264	                {self.arg_names[i]: v for i, v in enumerate(arg_values)}
265	                for arg_values in batch_kwargs
266	                if isinstance(arg_values, (list, tuple))
267	                and len(self.arg_names) - len(self.arg_defaults)
268	                <= len(arg_values)
269	                <= len(self.arg_names)
270	            ]
271	            # Ensure to raise an exception if the number of arguments mismatch
272	            if len(batch_kwargs) != num_programs:
273	                raise Exception("Given arguments mismatch the SGL function signature")
274	
275	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
276	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
277	            n=n,
278	            stop=stop,
279	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
280	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
281	            temperature=temperature,
282	            top_p=top_p,
283	            top_k=top_k,
284	            min_p=min_p,
285	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
286	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
287	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
288	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
289	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
290	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
291	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
292	        )
293	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
294	        return run_program_batch(
295	            self,
296	            backend,
297	            batch_kwargs,
298	            default_sampling_para,
299	            num_threads,
300	            progress_bar,
301	            generator_style=generator_style,
302	        )
303	
304	    def trace(self, *, backend=None, **kwargs):
305	        from sglang.lang.tracer import trace_program
306	
307	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
308	        return trace_program(self, kwargs, backend)
309	
310	    def cache(self, backend=None):
311	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import cache_program
312	
313	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
314	        return cache_program(self, backend)
315	
316	    def __call__(self, *args, **kwargs):
317	        from sglang.lang.tracer import TracingScope
318	
319	        tracing_scope = TracingScope.get_current_scope()
320	        if tracing_scope is None:
321	            return self.run(*args, **kwargs)
322	        else:
323	            kwargs["backend"] = tracing_scope.tracer_state.backend
324	            return self.trace(*args, **kwargs)

327	class SglExpr:
328	    node_ct = 0
329	
330	    def __init__(self):
331	        self.node_id = SglExpr.node_ct
332	        self.prev_node = None
333	        self.pid = None
334	        SglExpr.node_ct += 1
335	
336	    def __add__(self, other):
337	        if isinstance(other, str):
338	            other = SglConstantText(other)
339	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr)
340	
341	        return self.concatenate_ir(self, other)
342	
343	    def __radd__(self, other):
344	        if isinstance(other, str):
345	            other = SglConstantText(other)
346	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr), f"{other}"
347	
348	        return self.concatenate_ir(other, self)
349	
350	    def concatenate_ir(self, a, b):
351	        if isinstance(a, SglExprList):
352	            if isinstance(b, SglExprList):
353	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + b.expr_list)
354	            else:
355	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + [b])
356	        elif isinstance(b, SglExprList):
357	            return SglExprList([a] + b.expr_list)
358	
359	        return SglExprList([a, b])
360	
361	    def print_graph_dfs(self):
362	        ret = [""]
363	        visited = set()
364	
365	        def dfs_print(x):
366	            if x is None or x in visited:
367	                return
368	            visited.add(x)
369	
370	            # Print dependency
371	            if x.prev_node is not None:
372	                dfs_print(x.prev_node)
373	
374	            if isinstance(x, SglExprList):
375	                for y in x.expr_list:
376	                    dfs_print(y)
377	            # elif isinstance(x, SglRole):
378	            #    dfs_print(x.expr)
379	            elif isinstance(x, SglVariable):
380	                dfs_print(x.source)
381	
382	            # Print the node itself
383	            if isinstance(x, (SglFork, SglGetForkItem)):
384	                ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = {x}\n"
385	            else:
386	                if x.prev_node is not None:
387	                    ret[0] += (
388	                        f"%{x.node_id} = %{x.prev_node.node_id} + " + str(x) + "\n"
389	                    )
390	                else:
391	                    ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = " + str(x) + "\n"
392	
393	        dfs_print(self)
394	        return ret[0]
395	
396

619	class SglSeparateReasoning(SglExpr):

623

625	        self.name = None
626	        self._process_expr(expr)

628	    def process_name_for_reasoning(self, name):
629	        if not name:
630	            raise ValueError("name must be provided")
631	        return f"{name}_reasoning_content"
632	
633	    def _process_expr(self, expr):
634	        if isinstance(expr, SglGen):
635	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
636	        elif isinstance(expr, SglSelect):
637	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
638	        elif isinstance(expr, SglExprList):
639	            for x in expr.expr_list:
640	                self._process_expr(x)
641	
642	    def __repr__(self):
643	        return f"SeparateReasoning(model_type={self.model_type}, name={self.name})"

14	"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""
15	
16	import logging
17	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Union
18	
19	# sre_parse is deprecated in Python 3.11+, use re._parser instead
20	try:
21	    import re._parser as sre_parse
22	except ImportError:
23	    import sre_parse  # Python < 3.11
24	
25	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-6
26	TOP_K_ALL = 1 << 30
27	
28	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
29	
30	
31	class SamplingParams:
32	    """
33	    The sampling parameters.
34	
35	    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
36	    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
37	    for the documentation.
38	    """
39	
40	    def __init__(
41	        self,
42	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
43	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
44	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
45	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
46	        temperature: float = 1.0,
47	        top_p: float = 1.0,
48	        top_k: int = -1,
49	        min_p: float = 0.0,
50	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
51	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
52	        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
53	        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
54	        n: int = 1,
55	        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
56	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
57	        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
58	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
59	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
60	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
61	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
62	        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
63	        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
64	        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
65	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
66	        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
67	    ) -> None:
68	        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
69	        self.stop_strs = stop
70	        if stop_token_ids:
71	            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
72	        else:
73	            self.stop_token_ids = None
74	        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
75	        self.temperature = temperature
76	        self.top_p = top_p
77	        self.top_k = top_k
78	        self.min_p = min_p
79	        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
80	        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
81	        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
82	        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
83	        self.regex = regex
84	        self.n = n
85	        self.json_schema = json_schema
86	        self.ebnf = ebnf
87	        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
88	        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
89	        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
90	        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
91	        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
92	        self.custom_params = custom_params
93	        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
94	        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
95	        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed
96	
97	        # Process some special cases
98	        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
99	            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
100	            self.temperature = 1.0
101	            self.top_k = 1
102	        if self.top_k == -1:
103	            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary
104	
105	    def verify(self, vocab_size):
106	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
107	            raise ValueError(
108	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
109	            )
110	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
111	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
112	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
113	            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
114	        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
115	            raise ValueError(
116	                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
117	            )
118	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
119	            raise ValueError(
120	                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
121	                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
122	            )
123	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
124	            raise ValueError(
125	                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
126	            )
127	        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
128	            raise ValueError(
129	                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
130	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
131	            )
132	        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
133	            raise ValueError(
134	                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
135	                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
136	            )
137	        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
138	            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
139	                raise ValueError(
140	                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
141	                )
142	            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
143	                raise ValueError(
144	                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
145	                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
146	                )
147	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
148	            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
149	                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
150	                    raise ValueError(
151	                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
152	                        f"{token_id}."
153	                    )
154	
155	        grammars = [
156	            self.json_schema,
157	            self.regex,
158	            self.ebnf,
159	        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
160	        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
161	            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
162	
163	    def normalize(self, tokenizer):
164	        # Process stop strings
165	        if self.stop_strs is None:
166	            self.stop_strs = []
167	            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
168	        else:
169	            if isinstance(self.stop_strs, str):
170	                self.stop_strs = [self.stop_strs]
171	
172	            stop_str_max_len = 0
173	            for stop_str in self.stop_strs:
174	                if tokenizer is not None:
175	                    stop_str_ids = tokenizer.encode(stop_str, add_special_tokens=False)
176	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
177	                else:
178	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str))
179	            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len
180	
181	        # Process stop regex strings
182	        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
183	            self.stop_regex_strs = []
184	            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
185	        else:
186	            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
187	                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]
188	
189	            stop_regex_max_len = 0
190	            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
191	                stop_regex_max_len = max(
192	                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)
193	                )
194	
195	            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len
196	
197	
198	# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
199	# to be buffered to match the input regex string
200	# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one
201	# token
202	def get_max_seq_length(regex_str: str):
203	    return _max_length_from_subpattern(sre_parse.parse(regex_str))
204	
205	
206	MAX_LEN = 2**30
207	
208	
209	def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
210	    total = 0
211	    for token, value in subpattern:
212	        if token in {
213	            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
214	            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
215	            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
216	        }:
217	            total += 1
218	        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
219	            # EG: (a\d+) ->
220	            # [(SUBPATTERN,
221	            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
222	            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
223	            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
224	            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
225	        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
226	            _, branches = value
227	            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
228	        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
229	            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
230	            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
231	                total += MAX_LEN
232	            else:
233	                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
234	        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
235	            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
236	            # length
237	            total += 0
238	        else:
239	            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")
240	
241	            total += MAX_LEN
242	
243	    return total
244

# Copyright 2023-2024 SGLang Team
